story_prompt: make a lecture on js class

scenes.text: JavaScript classes are templates for creating objects. They provide a more convenient syntax to create object and deal with inheritance, similar to class-based languages. A class encapsulates data and functions that can be shared among the objects created from it, improving code structure and readability. This class-based approach is especially useful in larger applications with a need for organized and reusable code, making maintenance easier.

JavaScript class inheritance is a powerful feature that allows a class to extend another class. By using the 'extends' keyword, you can create a new class that inherits properties and methods from an existing class, enabling the reuse of code. Inheritance promotes a hierarchical class structure and supports code reusability and extension without modifying existing code, which is fundamental in object-oriented programming.
